anima.name: Anima
desc: Magically awakened or fragments of lingering emotion, “anima” is considered an umbrella term for those wayward spirits that weren't able to make their final departure from the Material Plain peacefully; that final fragment of their being determines what they become in their life's encore. There's nothing they want more than to move on from this life, even if it means tearing this world apart.
anima.age: Anima are ambiguously alive. They show no signs of aging, and they are immune to any effects that would age them. You physically resemble your form when you died. You can be freshly reanimated or thousands of years old.
anima.alignment: Anima tend toward neutrality, but always contain a sliver of memories of their previous life.
anima.size: Your body is translucent, but any close observer can easily make out your race in life. Your size is Small or Medium, depending on who you were.
anima.languages: Common and any additional languages you knew in life.

abilities.name: Incorporealis
abilities.desc: Whenever an attack or spell would hit you, you may use your reaction to have the attack pass through you completely. The damage of the attack or spell becomes 1d10 @necrotic damage, instead of the damage it would normally do. You may use this trait once every long rest.

abilities.name: Genus
abilities.desc: Your story in life determines who you are in death. Your visible body always resembles a playable race, but you don't gain any of its benefits. Choose one of the subraces below.

umbra.name: Umbra
umbra.desc: A light blue, translucent spirit most often found in abandoned houses. It is a spirit of regret most often sprouting from peaceful passings. Hundreds or thousands of years of natural energy accumulating around a faded mind creates what is known to man as an “umbra”, retaining but a sliver of their memories in life, cursed to forever wander the world, remembering naught but their remorse.

abilities.name: Ingredior
abilities.desc: You may move inside the space of another creature, and end your turn there. If you end your turn in the same space as a creature, unless it is an Undead, you and the creature both take 1d10 @psychic damage. You cannot use this trait if you are carrying anything that is not incorporeal.

abilities.name: Nulla Forma
abilities.desc: You are able to move through walls and objects made of non-magical wood, stone and metal, treating it like difficult terrain. You may do this a number of times equal to your CON modifier, and only for a maximum distance of 5ft. multiplied by half your CON modifier, rounded down, each time. If you end your turn inside a wall or object, you take 1d10 @force damage, and are shunted out towards the side of the wall you entered it from. You cannot use this trait if you are carrying anything that is not incorporeal. Any expended uses of the nulla forma trait regenerate after finishing a long rest.

abilities.name: Aetherius
abilities.desc: As a bonus action, you may make any one object you touch incorporeal. For 10 minutes, the affected object becomes translucent, signaling it is now incorporeal. For the duration, no creature except yourself or other incorporeal beings can affect or be affected by the incorporeal object. The object can pass through walls only if you are holding or touching it. You may use this trait once per long rest.

larua.name: Larua
larua.desc: Black, putrid ghosts that border on monstrosity, the larua are spirits of vengeance, seeking revenge against a cause unbeknownst to even themselves. Some call them corrupted umbras, the regret of ones passing burning and flaring into deep-rooted hatred. They are harbingers of death and heralders of the end, unwilling and unable to rest until they have avenged that they held dear once, in life.

abilities.name: Dominus Noctus
abilities.desc: You are a master of blending into the shadows. You gain proficiency in the Stealth (DEX) skill. Additionally, you gain advantage on Stealth (DEX) rolls at night.

abilities.name: Perimo
abilities.desc: Your mere presence gives other creatures goushonkhebumps. Whenever a creature other than you within 5ft. of you is hit with a melee attack, the creature takes an additional 1d4 @psychic damage. This increases to 1d6 at 5th level, 1d8 at 9th level, 1d10 at 13th level and 1d12 at 17th level.

abilities.name: Pestis
abilities.desc: You gain resistance to @necrotic damage.

phantasma.name: Phantasma
phantasma.desc: An age-old spirit that has lost its original form after many years of wandering. Sometimes called carrion ghosts, or parasite spirits, they seek refuge in corpses of the freshly deceased, breathing new “life” into them. They bond with this new body and use it as their own, but the decay doesn't stop; a phantasma's greatest struggle is to maintain its body. Because as the decay spreads and the body falls apart, the phantasma is ripped apart as well.

abilities.name: Vita Contractus
abilities.desc: You inhabit the dead body of a recently deceased humanoid. In order to maintain your body, you must spend at least 1 hour per day outside of rest repairing decay, or suffer 1 level of exhaustion. Additionally, you may add +1 per level of exhaustion to any Intimidation (CHA) check.

abilities.name: Auctoritas
desc: Although your spirit is bound to your host body, you can temporarily leave it. Your spirit can survive for 10 minutes outside your host body, up to 30ft. away. If you attempt to move further than 30ft. away from your host body, you will be blocked, as if by an invisible and impenetrable wall. During this time, you are deafened and incorporeal; this means you cannot affect or be affected by creatures. Your spirit appears as a small, light green orb that sheds dim light in a 5ft. radius. You can move through gaps as small as 1ft. without squeezing.

Additionally, as an action, you may attempt to possess one living creature (unless it is an Undead) you can see that is no more than 30ft. away from your host body. It must succeed on a DC 10 + CON + proficiency CON saving throw, or be possessed for 1 minute. During combat, any unwilling creature you are possessing may repeat the saving throw at the start of each of their turns, ending the possessions on itself on a success. When the possession ends, the creature previously possessed is knocked prone, and you are instantaneously returned to your host body.

If you successfully possess a creature, the creature is incapacitated, and cannot move or speak, but is not deprived of awareness. You have full control over the creature's body and are able to perceive everything through its senses. Starting at 5th level, you also gain access to its memories, as if the creature is affected by a successful @detect thoughts spell. You keep your INT, WIS, CHA scores, personality and alignment. All remaining statistics are replaced by the creature's. The creature's eyes glow a faint green color. You may make a melee or ranged weapon attack the creature would be able to make, on your own turn. You may only take one action this way, and effects that give you or the possessed creature extra actions on its turn do not work. Additionally, as a bonus action, you may inflict pain upon the creature's mind. It must succeed on a DC 10 + CON + proficiency CHA saving throw or take 1d8 @psychic damage, increased to 2d8 on 6th level, 3d8 on 11th level and 4d8 on 16th level.

If you force the creature to move more than 30ft. away from your host body while you are still possessing it, or if the creature is knocked unconscious, your spirit will be ripped out, dealing 1d10 @force damage to both you and the creature. If the creature takes damage and is reduced to 0 hitpoints, your spirit will be ripped out and you will take damage equal to 1d10 + the remaining damage @force damage. (Example: if a creature has 2 hitpoints and takes 4 damage while you are possessing it, you take 1d10 + 2 @force damage).
You may use this trait once per long rest.

abilities.name: Mors in Vita
abilities.desc: You gain resistance to @poison damage.

catfolk.name: Catfolk
desc: The mysterious catfolk are an all-female half cat hybrid people that dwell in secluded places. Having distant ancestors in the fey and beast corners, as well as an ancient, long-lost civilization, they distinguish themselves with their short builds and feline features. Their villages are spread out across any form of terrain, albeit well-hidden no matter what tribe you come across. In fact, the majority of the catfolk are secluded in such a fashion that often they have no contact with the outside world. The catfolk are usually nothing more than myths for anyone who comes across them, and they're often mistaken as fey or Tabaxi. Those who do find the catfolk villages inside the foliage of the feywild will most likely meet their end by sheer savagery.
catfolk.age: A catfolk claims adulthood at the age of 12. They rarely live beyond 75 years.
catfolk.alignment: Wherever the catfolk live, they live in complete harmony with their environment, be it heavily forested areas, mountains or beachsides. Most catfolk are neutral.
catfolk.size: Catfolk are small, but ferocious in battle. The average catfolk stands at 3ft. to 4ft. in height. Your size is Small.
catfolk.languages: You can speak, read and write Catspeak, a primitive language that is hard to describe in script. Due to isolation, catfolk rarely come in contact with anyone that speaks Common. You may add Common to your list of languages at your DM's discretion.

abilities.name: Camouflage
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in the Stealth (DEX) skill.

abilities.name: Pack Tactics
abilities.desc: You may add +2 to any attack roll whenever there is an ally within 5ft. of the target, and the ally is not incapacitated. This increases to +3 from 10th level.

cervitaur.name: Cervitaur
desc: A mystical sister family of the more well-known centaurs, cervitaurs are far descendants of dream spirits from the feywild. The four legs of a fawn adorned by a humanoid torso make for an agile and fast-witted creature that finds its home in the many types of heavy foliage. They trek around the feywild in their mostly solitary life. Although shy and easily scared, they are true friends of nature, nourishing their home with their dreamy magic.
cervitaur.age: Cervitaurs mature and age at about the same rate as humans, reaching adulthood at around 18 years old, and living to around 80 years on average.
cervitaur.alignment: Cervitaurs see themselves as a part of the environment. As nature adapts, so will they, and thus range from lawful to chaotic and from good to evil, based on the environment they grew up in.
cervitaur.languages: You can speak, read and write Common and Sylvan.

abilities.name: Dream Spirit
abilities.desc: You have a deep connection with the Plane of Dreams and are able to use its soothing power to put creatures to rest. As an action, you may point at a creature no more than 90ft. away from you. That creature falls into a magical slumber, as if affected by the @sleep spell. You may affect any creature with a CR equal to your level or lower. If the creature awakens by any means, the creature suffers 2d4 @psychic damage as a result. This increases to 2d6 at level 5, 2d8 at level 9, 2d10 at level 13 and 2d12 at level 17. You may use this trait a number of times equal to your WIS every long rest. Additionally, at the start of each of your turns, you recover 1 hitpoint for every sleeping creature within 30ft. of you.

abilities.name: Fawnish Build
abilities.desc: Your skittish nature and often lush environments has evolved you to be more agile. The distance you can jump in both long jumps and high jumps is doubled for you, and jumping from a standing position does not give you a penalty. Additionally, any climb that requires hands and feet is especially difficult for you because of your deer-like legs. Climbing costs 5ft. per ft. of movement climbed, instead of the usual 1ft. per ft.

abilities.name: Natural Camouflage
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in the Stealth (DEX) skill. Additionally, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Nature (WIS) or Survival (WIS).

dragonborn.name: Dragonborn
desc: The dragonborn walk proudly through a world that greets them with fearful incomprehension. Shaped by the dragons themselves, dragonborn originally hatched from dragon eggs as a unique race, combining the best attributes of dragons and humanoids.
dragonborn.age: Young dragonborn grow quickly. They walk hours after hatching, attain the size and development of a 10 year old human child by the age of 3, and reach adulthood by 15. They live to be around 80 years old.
dragonborn.alignment: Far descendants from dragons, their path is oft similar to that of their lineage. Metallic dragonborn are often good, while chromatic dragonborn are more cruel and savage.
dragonborn.size: Dragonborn are taller and heavier than humans, standing well over 6ft. tall. Your size is Medium.
dragonborn.languages: You can read, speak and write Common and Draconic.

abilities.name: Draconic Ancestry
abilities.desc: You are distantly related to a particular kind of dragon. Choose a type of dragon from the following:

abilities.name: Breath Weapon
abilities.desc: As an action, you may exhale destructive energy. It deals damage in an area according to your ancestry. All creature in the area must make a saving throw with DC 8 + CON + your proficiency, taking 2d6 damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The type of saving throw is determined by your ancestry. The damage of your breath weapon increases to 3d6 at level 6, 4d6 at level 11 and 5d6 at level 16. You may use this trait once per short or long rest.

abilities.name: Draconic Resistance
abilities.desc: You gain resistance to the damage type associated with your ancestry.

dwarf.name: Dwarf
desc: Kingdoms rich in ancient grandeur, halls carved into the roots of mountains, the echoing of picks and hammers in deep mines and blazing forges, a commitment to clan and tradition, and a burning hatred of goblins and orcs - these common threads unite all dwarves.
dwarf.age: Dwarves mature at the same rate as humans, but they're considered young until they reach the age of 50. On average, they live about 350 years.
dwarf.alignment: Accustomed to a life of hard work, most dwarves are lawful, further following the path of their clan in good or evil.
dwarf.size: Dwarves stand between 4ft. and 5ft. tall. Your size is Medium.
dwarf.languages: You can speak, read and write Dwarvish and Common. Dwarvish is full of hard consonants and guttural sounds, which spill over into whatever other language a dwarf might speak.

abilities.name: Darkvision
abilities.desc: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You are able to see in dim light within 60ft. of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness; only shades of grey.

abilities.name: Dwarven Resilience
abilities.desc: You gain advantage on saving throws against poison, and you gain resistance to @poison damage.

abilities.name: Dwarven Combat Training
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer and warhammer. Additionally, wearing heavy armor does not impose a penalty on your speed.

abilities.name: Tool Proficiency
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency with one of these artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.

abilities.name: Stonecunning
abilities.desc: Whenever you make a History (INT) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered to have expertise in the History (INT) skill, adding double your proficiency bonus instead of the normal scenario.

hill-dwarf.name: Hill Dwarf
hill-dwarf.desc: As a hill dwarf, you have keen senses, deep intuition, and remarkable resilience. The gold dwarves of Faerun in their mighty southern kingdom are hill dwarves, as are the exiled Neidar and the debased Klar of Krynn in the Dragonlance setting.

abilities.name: Dwarven Toughness
abilities.desc: Your hitpoint maximum is increased by a number equal to your level count.

mountain-dwarf.name: Mountain Dwarf
mountain-dwarf.desc: As a mountain dwarf, you're strong and hardy, accustomed to a difficult life in rugged terrain. You're probably on the tall side (for a dwarf), and tend toward lighter coloration. The shield dwarves of northern Faerun, as well as the ruling Hylar clan and the noble Daewar c1an of Dragonlance, are mountain dwarves.

abilities.name: Dwarven Armor Training
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency with light and medium armor.

elf.name: Elf
desc: Elves are a magical people of otherworldly grace, living in places of ethereal beauty, in the midst of ancient forests or in silvery spires glittering with faerie light, where soft music drifts through the air and gentle fragrances waft on the breeze. Elves love nature and magic, art and artistry, music and poetry.
elf.age: Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.
elf.alignment: Elves love freedom, variety, and self-expression, so they lean strongly towards the gentler aspects of chaos. They value and protect others' freedom as well as their own, and are good more often than not. Drow are an exception; their exile into the Underdark has made them vicious and dangerous. Drow are more often evil than not.
elf.size: Elves range from under 5 to over 6ft. tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.
elf.languages: You can speak, read and write Common and Elvish.

abilities.name: Darkvision
abilities.desc: Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You are able to see in dim light within 60ft. of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness; only shades of grey.

abilities.name: Fey Ancestry
abilities.desc: You gain advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic cannot put you to sleep.

abilities.name: Trance
abilities.desc: Elves do not sleep. Instead they meditate deeply, remaining semi-conscious, for 4 hours each day. The Common word for this meditation is “trance”. While meditating, you dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive after years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit a human would from 8 hours of sleep.

abilities.name: Keen Senses
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in the Perception (WIS) skill.

drow.name: Dark Elf / Drow
drow.desc: Descended from an earlier subrace of dark-skinned elves, the Drow were banished from the surface world for following the goddess Lolth down the path to evil and corruption. Now they have built their own civilization in the depths of the Underdark, patterned after the Way of Lolth. Also called dark elves. The drow have black skin that resembles polished obsidian and stark white or pale yellow hair. They commonly have very pale eyes (so pale as to be mistaken for white) in shades of lilac, silver, pink, red, and blue. They lend to be smaller and thinner than most elves.

abilities.name: Superior Darkvision
abilities.desc: The range of your darkvision trait is increased to 120ft.

abilities.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
abilities.desc: You gain disadvantage on attack rolls and Perception (WIS) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

abilities.name: Drow Magic
abilities.desc: You know the dancing lights cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you may cast the @faerie fire spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you may cast the @darkness spell with this trait. You do not need material components for either spell. You may cast both spells once every long rest. CHA is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

abilities.name: Drow Weapon Training
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency with rapiers, shortswords and hand crossbows.

eladrin.name: Eladrin
eladrin.desc: Eladrin are elves of the Feywild, a realm of perilous beauty and boundless magic. Using that magic, eladrin can step from one place to another in the blink of an eye, and each eladrin resonates with emotions captured in the Feywild in the form of seasons — affinities that affect the eladrin's mood and appearance. An eladrin's season can change, though some remain in one season forever.

abilities.name: Seasons
abilities.desc: Choose your season, or roll on the eladrin seasons table.

content: Immediately after you use your fey step trait, up to 2 creatures of your choice that you can see within 10ft. of you must succeed on a WIS saving throw or be charmed by you for 1 minute. If you or your companions deal damage to a creature charmed in this way, the effect ends.

Whenever you use your fey step trait, a creature of your choice that you can see with 5ft. of you before you teleport must succeed on a WIS saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn.

Whenever you use your fey step trait, you may touch a willing creature within 5ft. of you. That creature then teleports instead of you, appearing in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 30ft. of you.
Immediately after you use your fey step trait, each creature of your choice that you can see within 5ft. of you takes @fire damage equal to your CHA (minimum of 1).

abilities.name: Fey Step
abilities.desc: As a bonus action, you may magically teleport up to 30ft. to an unoccupied space you can see. Starting at 3rd level, your fey step trait gains an additional effect based on your season; if the effect requires a saving throw, the DC is 8 + CHA + proficiency. The effects are listed in the eladrin seasons table.

abilities.name: Trance
abilities.desc: Whenever you finish a trance, you may change your season. Additionally, you may gain up to 2 proficiencies with either a weapon or tool that you don't have, as you mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory. You retain them until you finish a long rest, at which point you may choose again.

eladrin.source: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

high.name: High Elf
high.desc: As a high elf, you have a keen mind and a mastery of at least the basics of magic. In many of the worlds of D&D, there are two kinds of high elves. One type (which includes the gray elves and valley elves of Greyhawk, the Silvanesti of Dragonlance, and the sun elves of the Forgotten Realms) is haughty and reclusive, believing themselves to be superior to non-elves and even other elves. The other type (including the high elves of Greyhawk, the Qualinesti of Dragonlance, and the moon elves of the Forgotten Realms) are more common and more friendly, and often encountered among humans and other races.

abilities.name: Cantrip
abilities.desc: You know 1 cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. INT is your spellcasting ability for it.

abilities.name: Elf Weapon Training
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow and longbow.

abilities.name: Extra Language
abilities.desc: You can read, speak and write one additional language of your choice.

sea.name: Sea Elf
sea.desc: Sea elves fell in love with the wild beauty of the ocean in the earliest days of the multiverse. While other elves traveled from realm to realm, the sea elves navigated the deepest currents and explored the waters across a hundred worlds. Today, they live in small, hidden communities in the ocean shallows and on the Elemental Plane of Water.

abilities.name: Sea Elf Training
abilities.desc: You have proficiency with the spear, trident, light crossbow, and net.

abilities.name: Child of the Sea
abilities.desc: You have a swimming speed of 30ft., and you can breathe air and water.

abilities.name: Friend of the Sea
abilities.desc: Using gestures and sounds, you can communicate simple ideas with any beast that has an innate swimming speed.

abilities.name: Extra Language
abilities.desc: You can speak, read and write Aquan.

sea.source: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

shadar-kai.name: Shadar-Kai
shadar-kai.desc: Sworn to the Raven Queen's service, the mysterious Shadar-kai venture into the Material Plane from the Shadowfell to advance her will. Once they were fey like the rest of their elven kin, and now they exist in a strange state between life and death. Eladrin and Shadar-kai are like reflections of each other: one bursting with emotion, the other nearly devoid of it.

abilities.name: Blessing of the Raven Queen
abilities.desc: As a bonus action, you may magically teleport up to 30ft. to an unoccupied space you can see. Starting at 3rd level, after you teleport in this way, you have resistance to all damage until the start of your next turn. During that time, you appear ghostly and translucent. You may use this trait an amount of times equal to your proficiency per long rest.

abilities.name: Necrotic Resistance
abilities.desc: You gain resistance to @necrotic damage.

abilities.name: Trance
abilities.desc: Whenever you finish a trance, you may gain up to 2 proficiencies with either a weapon or tool that you don't have, as you mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory. You retain them until you finish a long rest, at which point you may choose again.

shadar-kai.source: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

wood.name: Wood Elf
wood.desc: As a wood elf, you have keen senses and intuition, and your fleet feet carry you quickly and stealthily through your native forests. This category includes the wild elves (Grugach) of Greyhawk and the Kagonesti of Dragonlance, as well as the races called wood elves in Greyhawk and the Forgotten Realms. In Faerun, wood elves (also called wild elves, green elves, or forest elves) are reclusive and distrusting of non-elves.

abilities.name: Elf Weapon Training
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow and longbow.

abilities.name: Mask of the Wild
abilities.desc: You may attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist and other natural phenomena.

pallid.name: Pallid Elf
pallid.desc: The pallid elves are a mystical and insightful people with skin as pale as the surface of Exandria's largest moon. They emerged from the Pallid Grove this century and wander the world with childlike curiosity.

abilities.name: Incisive Sense
abilities.desc: You gain advantage on Investigation (WIS) and Insight (WIS) checks.

abilities.name: Blessing of the Moonweaver
abilities.desc: You know the @light cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you may cast the @sleep spell with this trait. Starting at 5th level, you may cast the @invisibility spell, targeting yourself, with this trait. You do not need material components for either spell. You may cast both spells once every long rest. WIS is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

mark-of-shadow.name: Mark of Shadow Elf
mark-of-shadow.desc: If you're an elf with the Mark of Shadow, you have this subrace, with the following traits.

abilities.name: Cunning Intuition
abilities.desc: Whenever you make an Stealth (DEX) or Performance (CHA) check, you may roll 1d4 and add the number rolled to the total ability check.

abilities.name: Wards and Seals
abilities.desc: You know the @minor illusion cantrip. Starting at 3rd level, you may also cast the @invisibility spell with this trait. You do not need material components for it. You may cast it once per long rest. CHA is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

abilities.name: Spells of the Mark
abilities.desc: If you have the spellcasting (or similar) class feature, you may add the following spells to the spell list of your spellcasting class:

mark-of-shadow.source: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

astral.name: Astral Elf
astral.desc: Because nothing ages in the Astral Plane, astral elves from that plane are thousands of years old, and their longevity gives them an unusual perspective on time. Whether they choose to live in quiet contemplation or strike out to explore the reaches of the multiverse, they tend to see things through the lens of time as having little or no meaning to them.

abilities.name: Astral Aspect
abilities.desc: Choose one of the following ability scores: INT, WIS or CHA. The chosen ability score becomes the spellcasting abilities for all cantrips and spells cast using this race's traits. This cannot be changed after character creation.

abilities.name: Astral Fire
abilities.desc: You know one of the following cantrips of your choice: @dancing lights, @light, or @sacred flame

abilities.name: Radiant Soul
abilities.desc: After succeeding on a death save, you may regain hitpoints equal to your proficiency bonus + your Astral Aspect modifier. You may use this trait once every long rest.

abilities.name: Trance
abilities.desc: Whenever you finish a trance, you may gain up to 2 proficiencies with either a weapon or tool that you don't have, as you mystically acquire these proficiencies by drawing them from shared elven memory. You retain them until you finish a long rest, at which point you may choose again.

human.name: Human
desc: In the reckonings of most worlds, humans are the youngest of the common races, late to arrive on the world scene and short-lived in comparison to dwarves, elves, and dragons. Perhaps it is because of their shorter lives that they strive to achieve as much as they can in the years they are given. Or maybe they feel they have something to prove to the elder races, and that's why they build their mighty empires on the foundation of conquest and trade. Whatever drives them, humans are the innovators, the achievers, and the pioneers of the worlds.
human.age: Humans reach adulthood in their teens and live less than a century.
human.alignment: Humans are molded by nature and nurture, and have no preference for alignment.
human.size: Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5ft. to well over 6ft. tall. Regardless of your position in that range, your size is Medium.
human.languages: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice. Humans typically learn the languages of other peoples they deal with, including obscure dialects. They are fond of sprinkling their speech with words borrowed from other tongues: Orc curses, Elvish musical expressions, Dwarvish military phrases, and so on.

abilities.name: Skills
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

abilities.name: Feat
abilities.desc: You gain 1 feat of your choice.

voxin.name: Voxin
desc: A vastly mysterious race of humanoid beings with fluffy, bushy tails and long foxy ears, the voxin are a hidden civilization inside the feywild. Unmatched in their cunning and swiftness in battle, they can track their prey from miles away without breaking a sweat. Unlike the more savage catfolk, the voxin are a highly intelligent race, capable in knowledge and battle alike. Only if they need something will they ever show themselves to the outside world.

The voxin are also a very versatile people. The one-tailed voxin are the biggest and strongest among them, providing food and protection for the entire city. The two-tailed voxin are hoarders of knowledge, isolating themselves in libraries to unlock secrets of natures unknown. The nine-tailed voxin are often smaller in stature, but their charms and quick wit sets them apart as persuasive hagglers. Together they form gigantic cities hidden in the feywild. Entire voxin cities have been reported completely vanishing after a rare sighting of them.
You don't find them. They find you.
voxin.age: Voxin reach physical maturity at an age of around 30, but their intellect reaches its peak at an age of as young as 8 years. Unfeigned by the concept of adulthood, voxin can live to be around 500 years old.
voxin.alignment: Voxin live in big cities with strict social structures, tending heavily towards lawful alignments. Most of them move towards trickery and evil.
voxin.languages: You can speak, read and write Common, Sylvan and one language of your choice.

abilities.name: Cunning
abilities.desc: You may treat any simple weapon as if it has the finesse property.

abilities.name: Darkvision
abilities.desc: Predator in both night and day, you have keen eyes in even the darkest parts of the Feywild. You are able to see in dim light within 60ft. of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness; only shades of grey.

abilities.name: Tail Count
abilities.desc: Voxin are a very versatile race of creatures, having developed a social structure based on the amount of tails an individual has. Over time, their evolution has separated them into distinct groups. Choose one of the subraces below.

abilities.name: Voxin Step
abilities.desc: Your foxlike traits give you foxlike reflexes as well. After taking an action on your turn, you may move up to half your base movement speed. This extra movement does not count toward the total you may move on your turn. Additionally, opportunity attacks have disadvantage against you.

one-tailed-voxin.name: One-tailed Voxin
one-tailed-voxin.desc: The one-tailed voxin are the brawn of the voxin race. Hunters, trackers and guardians for the great metropolises the voxin call their home, they are the most imposing among their kind. It's not uncommon for these brutes to possess more bestial traits, like patches of fur, or an enlongated snout. One-tailed voxin are the most common of the voxin.
one-tailed-voxin.size: You stand with the tallest among your kind, ranging from just under 5ft. to a little over 6ft. tall. Your size is Medium.

abilities.name: Apex Predator
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in the Survival (WIS) and Perception (WIS) skills. Additionally, you may use this trait to cast the @longstrider spell, targeting yourself, requiring no material components. You may use this trait once per long rest.

two-tailed-voxin.name: Two-tailed Voxin
two-tailed-voxin.desc: The two-tailed Voxin are the brains of the Voxin race. A well-known myth states that the two-tailed voxin are the origin of all vulpine creatures found in the realms. Possessing the shy nature of these creatures, only very rarely will you find one outside of the city, and even outside of the library are they rarely seen. They are hoarders of knowledge, possessing far more than the rest of their kind, unlocking secrets of arcane and eldritch natures alike.
two-tailed-voxin.size: The two-tailed Voxin embody the average of a Voxin. You range from 4ft. to no more than 5ft. Your size is Small or Medium.

abilities.name: Scholar of Nature
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in the Arcana (INT) and Nature (INT) skills. Additionally, you may use this trait to cast the @entangle spell, requiring no material components. You can use this trait once per long rest. INT is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

nine-tailed-voxin.name: Nine-tailed Voxin
nine-tailed-voxin.desc: The nine-tailed Voxin are the diplomats of the Voxin race. Vastly different from their peers; they are both predators and hoarders, but in a different pursuit. By far the rarest among their kind, most nine-tailed Voxin choose to live a nomadic life, outside the city, often finding their way around the world as entertainers, bards and merchants. Their short build and traits makes them incredible showmen and traders, but always be wary around them, because there's something enchanting about their charms. Perhaps a bit... too enchanting.
nine-tailed-voxin.size: The nine-tailed Voxin are short and often appear childlike, seemingly never reaching adulthood the way their one- and two-tailed peers do. They stand little under 3ft. to no more than 4ft. tall. Your size is Small.

abilities.name: Unmatched Charms
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in the Persuasion (CHA) and Intimidation (CHA) skills. Additionally, you may use this trait to cast the @charm person spell, requiring no material components. While casting the @charm person spell using this trait, the target gains disadvantage on its saving throw. You may use this trait once per long rest. INT is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

kenoa.name: Keno'a
desc: The mother of equality gazed upon the world, and saw it wasn't so. From the essences of imbalance, She created the keno'a, a Simic hybrid between multiple races, taking nothing but the most fitting properties. The keno'a stand as graceful and dexterous as the felines, but are capable of greater intelligence than most. Therefore, they are humanoids with feline features, made in Her image as the pinnacle of equality. However, after the banishment of their guardian deity, their numbers started dwindling, gradually losing their balanced form over the course of many generations of evolution. Today, most of the former keno'a are part of small tribes known as the catfolk, having lost most pride, wit and grace, though pure-blooded keno'a are alive today. Part of human society, or disguised as their peers, they have forgotten the teachings of their matriarch, yet hold some ability to subconsciously inspire Her teachings.
kenoa.age: The keno'a take part in a divine ritual beknownst only to them, allowing to be reborn several times. They shed the burdens of an aged body, being reborn as a child, whilst keeping their life experiences. Each of their lifetimes reaches to 90 years, and they can be reborn up to 8 times.
kenoa.alignment: The mind of a keno'a tends to subconsciously mirror their surroundings, becoming good in the face of evil, inciting chaos in the eyes of the law. Despite that, they are inspired towards neutrality.
kenoa.size: Your size is Small or Medium.
kenoa.languages: You can speak, read and write Catspeak and Common.

abilities.name: Equality
desc: You are a creation of the Goddess of Equality, your body a temple of balance, Her teachings bestowed upon you. You gain +1 to saving throws made with your 2 lowest ability scores. If any ability scores are tied for being lowest, choose one. This bonus increases to +2 at 7th level, and +3 at 14th level. As an action, you may steel your resolve further, doubling these bonuses for 1 minute.

Alternatively, you may usher balance onto other creatures under your Matriarch's teachings: as an action, you may choose one creature you can see within 30ft. of you, and balance them in Her light. For 1 minute, that creature gains a -1 penalty to attack rolls, saving throws and ability checks made with the creature's 2 highest ability scores. This penalty increases to -2 at 7th level, and -3 at 14th level. This effect is regarded as a curse, and can be removed by the appropriate effects and spells, such as the greater restoration spell.
You may use either of these traits once per long rest.

abilities.name: Feline Reflexes
abilities.desc: You gain +2 to Perception (WIS) checks, and +4 to Passive Perception. This does not count as being proficient in this skill. Additionally, when in a situation that would cause you to be surprised, you may roll 1d2. On a 2, you are not surprised.

half-orc.name: Half-orc
desc: When alliances between humans and orcs are sealed by marriages, half-orcs are born. Some half-orcs rise to become proud chiefs of orc tribes, their human blood giving them an edge over their full-blooded orc rivals. Some venture into the world to prove their worth among humans and other more civilized races. Many of these become adventurers, achieving greatness for their mighty deeds and notoriety for their barbaric customs and savage fury.
half-orc.age: Half-orcs mature a little faster than humans, reaching adulthood at around age 14. They age noticeably faster and rarely live longer than 75 years.
half-orc.alignment: Half-orcs inherit a tendency towards chaos from their orc parents and are not strongly inclined toward good. Half-orcs raised among orcs and willing to live out their lives among them are usually evil.
half-orc.size: Half-orcs are somewhat larger and bulkier than humans, and they range from 5 to well over 6ft. tall. Your size is Medium.
half-orc.languages: You can speak, read and write Common and Orc. Orc is a harsh, grating language with hard consonants. It has no script of its own, but is written in the Dwarvish script.

abilities.name: Relentless Endurance
abilities.desc: When you are reduced to 0 hitpoints but not killed outright, you may drop to 1 hitpoint instead. You may use this trait once per long rest.

abilities.name: Savage Attacks
abilities.desc: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you may roll 1 of the weapon's damage dice 1 additional time and add it to the extra damage of the hit.

half-dragon.name: Half-dragon
desc: Half-dragons are the result when a human woman and a polymorphed dragon get a child together. Despite having similar ancestry, half-dragons differ from dragonborn in that they possess key draconic traits dragonborn are often missing; a tail, wings, and overflowing draconic energy. Despite often have traits such as horns and more claw-shaped hands and feet, they still retain a fraction of the smooth skin of their mother. Excruciatingly long-lived, and torn in both mind and body between their draconic and human natures, their bodies aren't built to withstand the power they possess, with dangerous results...
half-dragon.age: Blessed, and at the same time cursed with the longevity and borderline immortality a dragon enjoys, half-dragons mature and age extremely slowly, with hundreds of years between your birth, adulthood and death. You reach adulthood, both physically and mentally, after around 200 years, and can live tens of thousands of years. Sadly, most half-dragons don't even make it to adulthood, due to the strains of nigh immortality.
half-dragon.alignment: Half-dragons are most often raised by their human mothers, and adapt to their alignment. Although some are pulled towards their draconic nature, most half-dragons tend to choose their own path.
half-dragon.languages: You can speak, read and write Common and Draconic.

abilities.name: Ancestral Knowledge
abilities.desc: You gain proficiency in the Arcana (INT) skill. Additionally, you gain advantage to any History (INT) check made for dragon-related objects or subjects.

abilities.name: Draconic Offspring
abilities.desc: You are a child of a dragon and a human. Your father was a type of dragon of your choice. You are resistant to a single type of damage associated with your dragon parent (e.g. if your father was a white dragon, you are resistant to @cold damage; if your father was a deep (purple) dragon, you are resistant to @psychic damage, etc.). This trait cannot give resistance to @bludgeoning, @piercing, @slashing or @force damage.

abilities.name: Dragon Traits
desc: You have a long tail extruding from behind you. You may attempt to grapple a creature with your tail. If you do, roll with disadvantage. Additionally, you can hold light objects with your tail (like a book or a torch). You cannot attack with your tail.
You also have small wings attached to the lower half of your back. In your unawakened form, you cannot use them to fly, and they don't get in the way of armor.

abilities.name: Awakening
abilities.desc: A dragon is a creature of might and power, resulting in conflicting natures inside your own body. Half-dragons often struggle to contain the raw, primal energy swirling in their veins. If you fall below half your maximum hitpoints or lower, you lose control and temporarily overflow your body with raw draconic energy. Your awakening lasts for 1 hour. After your awakening ends, you gain 1 level of exhaustion. You can only awaken once every long rest. You:

content.desc: Gain much bigger wings. You gain a flying speed of 60ft., but gain disadvantage on attack rolls if you attempt to attack while flying. If your armor is not made to accomodate wings, it will break.
Gain immunity to the type associated to you by your Draconic Offspring trait, instead of the resistance you normally have.
Gain advantage on CON and CHA checks and saving throws, but disadvantage on DEX and WIS checks and saving throws.
Lose any proficiency bonuses on attack rolls.

Lose hitpoints at the start of your combat turns. At the start of your turn, roll 1d2. On a 1, you suffer damage equal to half your level, rounded down. On a 2, you suffer damage equal to a quarter of your level, rounded down. The minimum damage you can suffer by this trait is 1. Outside of combat, you do not lose hitpoints.
No longer have disadvantage while trying to grapple a creature with your tail.
Gain improved darkvision. The range of your darkvision is extended to 120ft., and you gain 10ft. blindsight.

abilities.name: Darkvision
abilities.desc: You are able to see in dim light within 60ft. of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness; only shades of grey.

half-dragon.source: Azzurey's Destructive Dictionary of Draconic Discord
